description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?

This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. description: Symptom: The port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes when 802.1X clients attempt to authenticate on the switch. This results in authentication failures for both 802.1X and MAC Authentication Bypass (MAB) clients. The system logs show repeated crash messages such as the following: port-accessd crashed due to signal:11. Scenario: When the RADIUS server is configured to return a Session-Timeout attribute with value set to 0 and an 802.1X client attempts to authenticate on the switch, the port-accessd daemon repeatedly crashes. Workaround: Remove the Session-Timeout = 0 attribute from the RADIUS server configuration or configure a value greater than 0. Use the client inactivity timeout none in the role configuration instead.

description: Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams using static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port>. This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency.

description: Symptom: When uploading firmware using the Web UI or REST API, the system displays the following error message despite the firmware being successfully transferred to the device: There was an error uploading new firmware. Firmware image is invalid. Please enter a valid firmware image. Scenario: When logging console is enabled in the switch configuration and a user attempts to upload firmware through the Web UI or REST API, the upload process is completed, but an HTTP 500 error is generated. Workaround: Until upgrading to the fixed version, use one of the following methods: 1. Disable console logging by removing logging console from the configuration. Then attempt the firmware upload using the Web UI or REST API again. 2. Use TFTP for firmware upload by uploading the firmware using TFTP or SFTP instead of the Web UI or REST API. Example command: copy tftp/sftp://[server-ip]/[firmware-file] primary vrf mgmt.

description: Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in device-mode instead of client-mode as follows: interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device

Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in device-mode instead of client-mode as follows: interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable Scenario 1: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 enabled and DHCPv6 disabled: Disabling DHCPv4 and enabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected When both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 disabled and DHCPv6 enabled: Enabling DHCPv4 and disabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Scenario 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected

scenario: 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected

Scenario: This issue occurs when administrators make configuration changes using Aruba Central, either through Central template groups or the UI group multi-edit option. The DHCP Server configuration status may become stuck in a pending state. This issue can occur even when the configuration changes are completely unrelated to DHCP services. The pending status persists indefinitely, requiring manual intervention.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
